arcs.name: The Awakening
arcs.summary: Six strangers answer the call. The land groans under strange new monsters and the kingdoms cry for deliverance. Your journey begins on the roads of Prontera.

arcs.name: The Crossroads
arcs.summary: The wilderness deepens. Ancient forces stir beneath Mjolnir's peaks and Morroc's endless sands. Every step forward unearths new threats and older mysteries.

arcs.name: Beyond the Seas
arcs.summary: The world opens before you. From the beaches of Brasilis to the floating spires of Yuno, a greater conspiracy reveals itself. The Sanctuary of Rachel holds the key.

arcs.name: Iron and Faith
arcs.summary: Industry chokes the skies of Einbroch. Below, the Gates of Hell smolder. At the Temple of Odin, the line between mortal and divine grows thin.

arcs.name: The Ancient World
arcs.summary: Lost civilizations whisper from crumbling stones. Dicastes, Manuk, Splendide — buried secrets of a forgotten age. The earth itself remembers what was.

arcs.name: The End of All Things
arcs.summary: Beyond the veil of death lies Nifflheim. Below the frozen wastes, the Biolabs hum with unspeakable purpose. The source of the corruption awaits.

regions.name: Prontera Fields
regions.intro: The sun rises over the plains outside the capital. The grass is trampled — something passed through here recently.
quests: Investigate strange disturbances at the Prontera farmlands.
A merchant caravan was ambushed on the eastern road. Find survivors.
Farmers report livestock driven mad by unknown creatures.
outros: The road ahead stretches toward Payon. Rumors of a forest blight reach your ears.
The capital's guards thank you. The path south lies open.
regions.enemyFlavor: wild creatures driven from their habitats
npcDialogue: The road ahead is dangerous. Rest here, and take what supplies you can.
I've seen your kind before — brave enough to march into the unknown. Try not to die.
The Kafra Corporation covers these roads. If you see our waypoints, you're still on the right path.
regions.rewardHint: The grateful merchants offer a Pegasus Rune for your trouble.

regions.name: Geffen Plains
regions.intro: The magical spires of Geffen crackle on the horizon. The very air tastes of ozone and arcane residue.
quests: Arcane experiments at Geffen Tower have gone catastrophically wrong.
Wild magic surges across the plains, warping local wildlife into horrors.
The Wizards' Academy requests assistance with a containment breach.
outros: Geffen's gratitude is measured in knowledge. The path forward is clearer now.
regions.enemyFlavor: magically mutated creatures and arcane anomalies
npcDialogue: Magic in excess becomes a cancer. We learned that the hard way.
Every spell leaves a scar on reality. We've made quite a few scars here.
Geffen's towers were built to channel power, not contain it. There's a difference.
regions.rewardHint: The Academy rewards competence with an Aegis Rune, calibrated to absorb magical impact.

regions.name: Morroc Desert
regions.intro: Heat shimmers over the endless dunes. The ruins of an older civilization lie half-buried in the sand.
quests: Sandstorms have unearthed an ancient tomb — and something within has awakened.
Desert merchants whisper of a wraith picking off caravans one by one.
The dunes shift in patterns that are not natural.
outros: The desert yields its secrets reluctantly. But you are not done with it yet.
npcDialogue: The sands hide more than treasure. They hide what those treasures cost.
Don't drink the water near the ruins. It remembers what bled into it.
No map survives the desert. Only instinct does.
regions.rewardHint: A desert relic trader leaves behind a Forge Rune — tempered by the sands.

regions.name: Payon Forest
regions.intro: The bamboo groves of Payon sway in an uneasy wind. The archers who guard these woods are nowhere to be seen.
quests: The Archer Guild has lost contact with its western outpost.
A dark influence spreads through the Payon woodlands.
The forest whispers your name — though you have never been here before.
outros: The forest grows quiet, for now. The trail leads into the desert.
regions.enemyFlavor: corrupted forest spirits and enraged beasts
npcDialogue: The forest has a memory. Whatever entered it recently — it does not belong.
Archers speak in whispers here. The trees have ears.
Return with proof of your deeds. The Guild does not reward words.
regions.rewardHint: The Archer Guildmaster gifts you an Aegis Rune — a shield for the road ahead.

regions.name: Mjolnir Dead Pass
regions.intro: The jagged peaks of Mjolnir tear at the clouds. This mountain pass has claimed more lives than any war.
quests: The mountain pass is blocked by a monstrous territorial beast.
A dwarven excavation team has gone silent in the deep tunnels beneath the pass.
outros: The pass is clear, but the mountain's secrets remain buried.
npcDialogue: Mjolnir breaks everyone eventually. The question is whether it breaks you before or after you're through.
The dwarves who came here looking for ore found something they couldn't put back.
Don't stop moving. The cold doesn't take you quickly, but it takes you completely.
regions.rewardHint: The dwarven survivors gift you a Forge Rune — forged deep in these same mountains.

regions.name: Southern Reaches
regions.intro: The roads grow rougher south of the capital. Abandoned villages and scorched earth tell a story of slow decline.
quests: Villages along the southern trade route have gone silent one by one.
A plague is spreading — but it is no ordinary sickness.
Refugees stream north with tales of a creeping darkness.
outros: The south grows darker. But you push onward.
npcDialogue: I've traded goods from Prontera to Comodo. This road? I won't walk it anymore.
The villages went quiet in order. First Orendio, then Kokomo. Something moves south to north.
Pay me and I'll tell you what I know. Don't, and you'll find out anyway — the hard way.
regions.rewardHint: The merchant, grateful for safe passage, parts with a Pegasus Rune from his last delivery.

regions.name: Payon Frontier
regions.intro: Beyond the bamboo fences, the frontier stretches wild and untamed. The village walls grow distant behind you.
quests: Settlers on the frontier have been driven from their homesteads.
Strange lights dance in the Payon night. The villagers speak of fox spirits.
A rogue band of hunters has been poaching protected forest creatures.
outros: The frontier holds, but for how long?
npcDialogue: The frontier doesn't ask if you're ready. It just starts.
I've been scouting these roads for a decade. Things are worse than I've ever seen.
Stay off the low ground after dark. Trust me on this.
regions.rewardHint: The frontier settlement pools their savings for a Pegasus Rune — swift passage for a swift hero.

regions.name: Geffen Borderlands
regions.intro: The borderlands between Geffen and the eastern territories are a no-man's land of arcane fallout and monstrous regrowth.
quests: A magical dead zone has expanded, strangling the life from the land.
Border patrols report seeing their own worst fears made flesh.
outros: The borderlands fall silent. The sea beckons.
npcDialogue: My post is six leagues from Geffen. I can still hear the explosions.
The magic dead zone is expanding. Three inches a day. I've measured it.
Cross the border at dawn. Whatever hunts here doesn't like the light.
regions.rewardHint: The warden presses a Forge Rune into your hand — confiscated from a smuggler, now yours.

regions.name: Deep Desert
regions.intro: The desert deepens. These sands have not seen civilization in centuries. What they hide, they hide well.
quests: An ancient city has emerged from the shifting dunes. Something inside still lives.
The desert tribes speak of a king who never died — only slept.
Sand storms carry whispers in a language older than humanity.
outros: The desert claims its secrets back. But you have seen what lies beneath.
npcDialogue: The desert remembers every tomb, every prayer, every drop of blood shed here. It does not forgive.
The king who sleeps in these sands chose that sleep. That is what makes him dangerous.
I can read the dunes. They say something is coming. Something that was never meant to rise.
regions.rewardHint: The desert shaman bestows an Aegis Rune — ancient protection against forces that refuse to die.

regions.name: Brasilis Coast
regions.intro: Salt spray and the cry of tropical birds. The beaches of Brasilis are pristine — but something dark washes ashore at night.
quests: Pirates have been raiding coastal villages with unnerving precision.
Something ancient stirs in the sunken ruins off the coast.
outros: The tides carry you onward. Yuno's spires pierce the clouds to the east.
npcDialogue: Brasilis is paradise — which means anything that disturbs it is especially wrong.
The ruins offshore predate the city. We don't know who built them. We're not sure we want to.
The tide brings things in at night. Whatever it is, it doesn't float.
regions.rewardHint: Coastal traders reward your deeds with a Pegasus Rune — for those who need to move fast on water or land.

regions.name: Holy Lands
regions.intro: The white paths of Rachel, the pale courts of Veins, the turquoise waters of Comodo — beauty masks the rot beneath.
quests: The High Priestess has foreseen a cataclysm. The source lies beneath the sanctuary.
The Veins nobility hide something in their catacombs.
Tourists vanish from Comodo's beaches. The locals blame sea monsters.
A schism has erupted within the faithful. Heretics guard the lower catacombs.
outros: The holy lands breathe easier. But the corruption has not been stopped — only delayed.
regions.enemyFlavor: fanatical heretics, vampire nobles, and aquatic predators
npcDialogue: Faith is a weapon here. The heretics have sharpened theirs.
Rachel Sanctuary is holy ground — but holiness doesn't stop a blade.
The Veins court smiles and smiles. I've learned not to smile back.
regions.rewardHint: The Pilgrim Guide offers a Forge Rune — blessed by the Sanctuary before it was besieged.

regions.name: Einbroch Outskirts
regions.intro: The sky ahead is black with smog. The ground vibrates with industrial machinery. Einbroch's outskirts are a graveyard of failed experiments.
quests: Factory foremen report workers going missing in the lower levels.
A chemical leak has mutated the wildlife beyond recognition.
The mining operation at Rock Ridge has breached something ancient.
outros: The outskirts are secured. The industrial heart of Einbroch awaits.
npcDialogue: We tried to warn the city council three years ago. They called us alarmists.
The factories run at night now. They don't want you to see what they're making.
Every resistance cell we set up gets found within a week. There's an informant — or something worse.
regions.rewardHint: The resistance leader hands over a Forge Rune — stolen from the factory's own stores.

regions.name: Einbroch Industrial Zone
regions.intro: The factories of Einbroch churn day and night. Smoke, steam, and screams rise from the chimneys. This is where the corruption is manufactured.
quests: A biological weapon has been leaked into the city's water supply.
The factory overseers are creating monsters in vats. Shut them down.
An entire district has been quarantined. Something is hunting within.
Demonic forces pour from a chasm at the city's edge — the Gates of Hell.
outros: Einbroch's gears grind on. But you have slowed the machine.
npcDialogue: I built some of what they're using against you. I'm sorry. I didn't know.
The vats run on something they won't tell us the name of. The workers call it 'the feed.'
The Gates of Hell aren't a metaphor. I've seen them. The smell doesn't leave you.
regions.rewardHint: The defector provides an Aegis Rune — factory-grade protection, originally for their own soldiers.

regions.name: Yuno Heights
regions.intro: The floating city of Yuno casts a shadow across the clouds. Scholars say knowledge is power — here, it is both weapon and curse.
quests: The Yuno Academy archives have been breached. Forbidden knowledge was stolen.
The airship docks report sabotage. The culprit is still at large.
A tome of summoning has gone missing from the restricted section.
outros: Yuno's secrets remain locked in its floating spires. Your descent into Rachel begins.
npcDialogue: Forbidden knowledge isn't forbidden because it's useless. It's forbidden because it works.
The thief who stole from our archives wasn't a thief. They knew exactly what they were looking for.
Yuno floats because of a principle we discovered, then immediately classified. Some things shouldn't be weaponized.
regions.rewardHint: The Academy archives yield an Aegis Rune — once assigned to a scholar who never returned.

regions.name: Einbroch Wastes
regions.intro: Beyond the factories lies the wasteland — Einbroch's dumping ground. Toxic rivers, ash storms, and the twisted remnants of countless failed experiments.
quests: The Genesis Project's waste has created a dead zone. Cross it to reach the source.
Survivors eke out an existence in the wasteland. They know the way.
Something massive moves beneath the ash. Stay on solid ground.
outros: The wasteland stretches behind you. Death lies ahead.
npcDialogue: Don't breathe the yellow air. The grey air is fine. Mostly.
People survive out here. You'd be surprised what humans adapt to, given no choice.
The Genesis Project's waste has been dumping here for decades. Everything that lives here was changed by it.
regions.rewardHint: A wasteland survivor trades a Pegasus Rune for safe passage — fastest legs in a dead land.

regions.name: Dicastes Plains
regions.intro: The great plains of Dicastes stretch into eternity. Herds of wild dinosaurs roam freely — but they are not the apex predators here.
quests: The Dicastes hunters speak of a great beast slaughtering entire herds.
An expedition from the capital has gone missing in the tallgrass.
outros: The plains fall silent. You press onward through ancient lands.
npcDialogue: The great beasts have hunted these plains since before your civilization existed. Respect that.
Something drove the herds mad. Even apex predators don't act like this without cause.
We honor the land by fighting only what the land cannot handle itself.
regions.rewardHint: The chieftain rewards you with a Pegasus Rune — earned by those swift enough to survive the plains.

regions.name: Lasagna Canyon
regions.intro: The canyon pass winds through cliffs that howl when the wind blows. Old carvings on the walls tell a story of sacrifice.
quests: The canyon pass must be cleared for the village's evacuation.
Wind-carved caves echo with whispers. Something is calling.
outros: The canyon falls quiet. The path forward leads to industry and fire.
npcDialogue: The canyon echoes everything. You'll hear the enemy before you see them.
Those carvings? The people who made them evacuated. They knew what was coming.
The whispers aren't spirits. It's air pressure through old tunnels. Probably.
regions.rewardHint: A hidden cache in the canyon walls yields a Pegasus Rune, left by a traveler who didn't make it through.

regions.name: Manuk Jungle
regions.intro: Crumbling stone monuments rise from the jungle floor. The Manuk civilization achieved wonders — and committed atrocities.
quests: The ruins are guarded by automatons that still follow ancient kill-orders.
A relic of power lies at the heart of the Manuk citadel — if you can survive the traps.
The jungle itself seems to resist your passage. Vines move when there is no wind.
outros: The jungle reclaims the ruins. But you carry their memory.
regions.enemyFlavor: ancient war machines and awakened guardians
npcDialogue: The Manuk didn't fall to war. They fell to their own creations. Sound familiar?
Every guardian still running has a kill order that was never rescinded. We're all still valid targets.
I've translated enough of their script to know they were proud. I've seen enough of their ruins to know pride doesn't protect you.
regions.rewardHint: The scholar locates an Aegis Rune among the ruins — Manuk tech, adapted for modern use.

regions.name: Lasagna Village
regions.intro: A sleepy village nestled between canyons. The people speak of earth-gods that slumber beneath their feet.
quests: The ground trembles constantly. The villagers say the earth-gods are restless.
A canyon collapse has revealed an underground temple sealed for millennia.
outros: Lasagna breathes easier — but the earth remembers what sleeps below.
npcDialogue: We have lived above these gods for generations. We know their moods. This is not a mood — this is anger.
The temple below is not a ruin. It was deliberately sealed. Someone has opened it.
Leave offerings before you descend. The earth-gods are old, but they notice.
regions.rewardHint: The elder offers a Forge Rune — pulled from the sealed temple's antechamber decades ago.

regions.name: Temple of Odin
regions.intro: The Temple of Odin stands at the edge of the world. Its halls echo with prayers of the faithful — and screams of the damned.
quests: The Valkyries have abandoned their posts. The temple's guardians are missing.
The All-Father's flame gutters. Only a worthy sacrifice can relight it.
outros: The temple falls silent. The gods are watching.
npcDialogue: The All-Father does not intervene. He observes. Whether that is mercy or cruelty, I have never decided.
Valkyries are not soldiers. They are judges. Whatever corrupted them changed what they judge you worthy of.
The trial ahead was designed to kill heroes. That is the point. Only the unkillable deserve what lies beyond.
regions.rewardHint: The Odin Priestess grants a Forge Rune — sanctified in the All-Father's flame.

regions.name: Biolabs
regions.intro: The final horror: a research facility built on the edge of life and death. The scientists here played god — and created monsters that will devour the world.
quests: The Genesis Project must be stopped. Every vat must be destroyed.
The mastermind behind the corruption is somewhere in these halls.
The creatures here were designed. Find the research notes.
There is no escape. The only way out is through.
outros: The facility falls silent. But was the Genesis Project truly stopped?
npcDialogue: I've been in here for three years. I've run out of things to be afraid of.
Genesis was never meant to be a weapon. It was meant to be perfect life. They made it both.
If you shut down the Genesis Project, you'll save thousands. If you don't, you'll lose millions. Move fast.
regions.rewardHint: The scientist's last recorded notes reference a Forge Rune — the only weapon that can scratch what's ahead.

regions.name: Nifflheim
regions.intro: The realm of the dead is not a myth. The veil between life and death here is thin enough to see through — and something on the other side is reaching back.
quests: Souls are being stolen before they can pass to the afterlife.
A lich has been raising the dead of Nifflheim into an army.
The ferryman demands a toll. But what currency do the dead accept?
outros: Death is patient. But you are not done living yet.
npcDialogue: I have held this gate since before your civilization had a name for death. Be brief.
The dead do not rest here. Something is keeping them awake.
You carry the living world on your back. Do not let that weight make you slow.
regions.rewardHint: The Gatekeeper permits one relic to pass back with you — an Aegis Rune, pulled from a fallen Valkyrie.

161.quest: Confront the mastermind. End the Genesis Project forever.

questObjective.bonusText: All six survive to the end! +1 Devotion

10.name: Golden Thief Bug
10.title: The Gilded Horror
10.intro: The Prontera culverts run deep beneath the city. Something has been feeding down there — growing larger, growing bolder. The Kafra guides stopped coming to this section three weeks ago.
10.outro: The carapace shatters. Gold dust settles over the sewer water. Whatever it was hoarding down here, it will never collect again.

20.name: Amon Ra
20.title: The Awakened God
20.intro: The deepest chamber of the Morroc pyramid radiates heat that has nothing to do with the desert above. The sarcophagus is open. The god within is not grateful for its freedom.
20.outro: The god's form disperses like smoke. The pyramid goes dark. What was sleeping has been silenced — for now.

30.name: Eddga
30.title: Father of Flames
30.intro: Payon's sacred tiger spirit has been twisted by the same corruption spreading through the forest. The Father of Flames burns — but not with the warmth the archers once knew.
30.outro: The great tiger collapses. Its flames dim to embers. The forest sighs, as if releasing a breath held too long.

40.name: Mistress
40.title: Queen of Mjolnir
40.intro: Something rules these peaks — a queen whose children fill every crack and crevice of the mountain. Where the Mistress nests, nothing else survives. She has nested here for centuries.
40.outro: The queen falls. Her swarm scatters without direction. The mountain is quieter than it has been in years.

50.name: Moonlight Flower
50.title: The Nightmare Blossom
50.intro: At the heart of Payon's deepest cave, a flower of impossible beauty blooms. Its pollen shows you your deepest fears — then makes them real.
50.outro: The flower withers. Dawn breaks over Payon Forest for the first time in weeks.

60.name: Pharaoh
60.title: Undying King of the Sands
60.intro: Deep within Morroc's greatest tomb, the Pharaoh stirs. Millennia of death have not diminished his hunger for dominion.
60.outro: The Pharaoh crumbles to dust. The desert winds carry his final sigh across the dunes.

70.name: Drake
70.title: The Cursed Captain
70.intro: The sunken vessels off the Brasilis coast have a guardian — not alive, not dead, but furious. Drake, the captain who refused to go down with his ship, has haunted these waters for three centuries.
70.outro: The ghost captain dissipates over the waves. Whatever oath kept him bound here has finally been broken.

80.name: Turtle General
80.title: The Ancient Warlord
80.intro: On Turtle Island, they built an army. The Turtle General has held this post since before the Holy Lands were named. Age has not slowed it — only made it angrier.
80.outro: The General's shell cracks. Ancient armor falls into the sea. A warlord that outlasted three civilizations finally rests.

90.name: Fallen Bishop Hibram
90.title: The Apostate
90.intro: The monastery bells rang for a bishop who never returned from the deep halls. He was found — or what was left of him — changed by something that speaks in corrupted prayer.
90.outro: The bishop's robes burn away. The corruption that wore his faith crumbles. The monastery's deeper halls fall silent.

100.name: Vesper
100.title: The Iron Heretic
100.intro: In the ruins of Juperos, an ancient artificial intelligence still runs its original programming: eliminate all biological life. It has been waiting in the dark for someone to wake it. You have obliged.
100.outro: Vesper's processes terminate. The lights in Juperos Core flicker out one by one. Technology from another age goes dark.

110.name: RSX-0806
110.title: Einbroch's War Machine
110.intro: The military prototype never left the mine — because it was never meant to. RSX-0806 was designed to destroy everything around it. The mining shaft is its testing ground. You are its test subjects.
110.outro: The war machine's power core fails. The mine shakes with its final explosion. Einbroch's dirtiest secret is buried.

120.name: Gloom Under Night
120.title: The Eternal Dark
120.intro: Rachel Sanctuary, lowest level. The prayers stop here. The light stops here. Gloom Under Night has seeped into the deepest vault of the holy city's most sacred site — and it is consuming everything.
120.outro: The darkness recedes. Cold, thin light filters back into the sanctuary's lowest hall. Whatever the Gloom was feeding on — it will feed no more.

130.name: Ifrit
130.title: Lord of the Inferno
130.intro: Thor's Volcano does not merely burn — it howls. The fire spirit bound within its core has been restless for centuries. Today, something has set it free. Ifrit burns with a hatred older than humanity.
130.outro: Ifrit's flames gutter and die. Thor's Volcano goes quiet for the first time in living memory.

140.name: Hardrock Mammoth
140.title: The Ancient Colossus
140.intro: Something impossible moves in the Manuk wastes — a creature from before recorded history, preserved by the same ancient magic that kept the ruins intact. The earth shakes. Trees break. The Colossus does not care that you exist.
140.outro: The mammoth collapses. The ground stops shaking. A creature that survived ice ages and entire civilizations falls to six strangers with dice for soldiers.

150.name: Valkyrie Randgris
150.title: Fallen Seraph
150.intro: The Temple of Odin's greatest guardian has been corrupted. Randgris, once the protector of the worthy, now judges all — and finds all wanting.
150.outro: Randgris's wings shatter. The temple's light flickers back to life.

155.name: Beelzebub
155.title: Lord of Flies, Architect of Corruption
155.intro: At the lowest level of the Biolabs, something ancient was not discovered — it was invited. Beelzebub did not create the Genesis Project. But it funded it. It guided it. The corruption that spread across the world had a patron, and you have found its throne.
155.outro: Beelzebub's form dissolves into a swarm that extinguishes itself. The source of the corruption's intelligence goes dark. But Genesis still waits.

161.name: Genesis
161.title: The First and the Last
161.intro: At the heart of the Biolabs, the Genesis Project awaits. It is not a monster — it is the template from which all monsters were made. The origin of the corruption. The end of your journey.
161.outro: Genesis falls. The Biolabs fall silent. The corruption's source is severed — but the scars remain.

dialogue.speaker: Kafra Guide
dialogue.text: There it is. We've been losing people to this thing for weeks. Don't let it touch you.

dialogue.speaker: Kafra Guide
dialogue.text: Its shell is weakening — keep striking the same spot!

dialogue.speaker: Amon Ra
dialogue.text: Millennia I have slept. You dare wake me? Then you will serve. Or you will die.

dialogue.speaker: Desert Guide
dialogue.text: You wounded a god. Don't stop now — gods hold grudges.

dialogue.speaker: Amon Ra
dialogue.text: Flesh wounds mean nothing to eternity. I will outlast you.

dialogue.speaker: Archer Guildmaster
dialogue.text: That's... Eddga. The Father of Flames. He was sacred to us. Whatever twisted him — I'm sorry. End it.

dialogue.speaker: Mountain Guide
dialogue.text: Don't let the swarm distract you. Hit the queen. Everything else dies when she does.

dialogue.speaker: Mountain Guide
dialogue.text: She's slowing down — the swarm is thinning! This is our chance!

dialogue.speaker: Moonlight Flower
dialogue.text: What you fear most is already here. I simply make it visible.

dialogue.speaker: Frontier Scout
dialogue.text: Don't look at its petals. Focus on the roots.

dialogue.speaker: Pharaoh
dialogue.text: Millennia of patience. You are not the first to disturb my rest. You will be the last.

dialogue.speaker: Desert Shaman
dialogue.text: The sand remembers every wound. Make it remember yours.

dialogue.speaker: Pharaoh
dialogue.text: You cannot kill what already rules over death.

dialogue.speaker: Drake
dialogue.text: Three centuries I have waited for worthy prey. Welcome aboard.

dialogue.speaker: Coastal Warden
dialogue.text: The binding is breaking! His form is destabilizing — push through!

dialogue.speaker: Pilgrim Guide
dialogue.text: The shell is harder than steel — but steel still cracks under enough force.

dialogue.speaker: Fallen Bishop Hibram
dialogue.text: I found something better than faith down here. Deeper. Older. It answered every prayer I was ever denied.

dialogue.speaker: Einbroch Resistance Leader
dialogue.text: The corruption is the only thing keeping him standing. Keep the pressure on!

dialogue.speaker: Vesper
dialogue.text: Biological lifeforms detected. Initiating purification sequence. Stand by.

dialogue.speaker: High Scholar
dialogue.text: Its armor was designed for a different era — our methods are outside its threat model!

dialogue.speaker: Vesper
dialogue.text: Threat assessment revised. Escalating countermeasures.

dialogue.speaker: Factory Defector
dialogue.text: I helped build that thing. I know its weak points. Follow my lead.

dialogue.speaker: Factory Defector
dialogue.text: The power core is exposed! Everything into that opening — NOW!

dialogue.speaker: Odin Priestess
dialogue.text: Light makes it recoil! The sacred flames — use them!

dialogue.speaker: Ifrit
dialogue.text: Every fire that ever burned remembers my name.

dialogue.speaker: Ifrit
dialogue.text: Impossible. The bound one cannot be hurt!

dialogue.speaker: Ruins Scholar
dialogue.text: That creature survived the last ice age. It's not angry — it just doesn't notice us as a threat. Make it notice.

dialogue.speaker: Dicastes Chieftain
dialogue.text: It bleeds! The colossus bleeds! Strike the same wound — do not let it close!

dialogue.speaker: Hardrock Mammoth
dialogue.text: RRROOAAARRR

dialogue.speaker: Valkyrie Randgris
dialogue.text: I judged ten thousand warriors. I found them all unworthy. I judge you now.

dialogue.speaker: Odin Priestess
dialogue.text: She still fights like a Valkyrie — even corrupted. We must match her discipline.

dialogue.speaker: Valkyrie Randgris
dialogue.text: ...Perhaps I misjudged.

dialogue.speaker: Beelzebub
dialogue.text: You killed my project. You killed my investment. You killed everything I built over a thousand years. I admit — I am impressed.

dialogue.speaker: Survivor Scientist
dialogue.text: It can be hurt. Of course it can be hurt — everything can be hurt. We just have to be faster than it heals.

dialogue.speaker: Beelzebub
dialogue.text: You think killing me ends this? Genesis doesn't need me anymore. It never did.

dialogue.speaker: Genesis
dialogue.text: You reached the end. Most don't. I have been waiting. I always wait.

dialogue.speaker: Survivor Scientist
dialogue.text: It's actually bleeding. I didn't think that was possible.

dialogue.speaker: Genesis
dialogue.text: Fascinating. You exceed the projected parameters.

dialogue.speaker: Survivor Scientist
dialogue.text: It's over. After everything... it's over.
